channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korean anti-corruption agency asks police to take over efforts to detain impeached Yoon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s anti-corruption agency has requested that police take over efforts to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol after its investigators failed to bring him to custody following a standoff with the presidential security service last week.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: France's former President Sarkozy standing trial over alleged campaign funding by Libya's Gadhafi
item.description: France’s former President Nicolas Sarkozy goes on trial Monday over alleged illegal financing of his 2007 presidential campaign by the government of late Libyan leader Moammar Gadhafi.
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Vaux-Montagny And Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Afghans arrive in the Philippines to complete visa processing for resettlement in US
item.description: A group of Afghan nationals have arrived in the Philippines to process special immigrant visas for their resettlement in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Favila And Eileen Ng, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Indonesia launches free meals program to feed children and pregnant women to fight malnutrition
item.description: Indonesia’s new government has begun an ambitious project to fight malnutrition by feeding nearly 90 million children and pregnant women that is expected to cost $28 billion through 2029, although critics question whether the program is affordable.
item.dc:creator: Niniek Karmini And Dita Alangkara, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Canadian PM Justin Trudeau to announce resignation as early as Monday: report
item.description: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, facing dwindling popularity in his country, is expected to resign this week, according to the Globe and Mail. He took office in 2015.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Heavy snow brings widespread disruption across the UK and Germany
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Heavy snow and freezing rain brought widespread disruption across Europe on Sunday, particularly in the U.K. and Germany, with several major airports forced to suspend flights. With the weather set to stay inclement on Sunday in the U.K.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS and MONIKA SCISLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shigemi Fukahori, who survived Nagasaki bombing and spent his life advocating for peace, dies at 93
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Shigemi Fukahori, a survivor of the 1945 Nagasaki atomic bombing, who devoted his life to advocating for peace and campaigning against nuclear weapons, has died. He was 93. Fukahori died at a hospital in Nagasaki, southwestern Japan, on Jan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader urges supporters to demonstrate to force Maduro to leave office
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado on Sunday urged supporters to demonstrate across the country, saying in a video message that President Nicolás Maduro will not step down on his own and they “must make him leave.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China fires shot across Trump's bow with defense sanctions
item.description: China’s decision this week to slap several major U.S. defense firms with penalizing trade measures is being viewed as a “shot across the bow” ahead of President-elect Trump’s inauguration. The move — targeting defense contractors Raytheon, Boeing, General Dynamics and Lockheed Martin — adds to U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: A soccer-loving nun from Brazil tops list of world's oldest living person at nearly 117
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A soccer-loving nun from Brazil is believed to have become the world's oldest living person at nearly 117 following the recent death of a woman from Japan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli PM office denies reports that Hamas forwarded list of hostages to release in event of deal
item.description: Israel denied reports that Hamas forwarded a list of 34 hostages it could release in exchange for a cease-fire Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More than 30 dolphins have died since an oil spill near southern Russia, experts say
item.description: A dolphin rescue center in Russia says 32 dolphins have died since fuel oil spilled out of two storm-stricken tankers three weeks ago in the Kerch Strait near the Russia-occupied Crimean Peninsula.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korean protesters brave cold to demand Yoon's ouster as detention deadline looms
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Hundreds of South Koreans, bundled up against freezing temperatures and snow, rallied overnight into Sunday near the residence of impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol, calling for his ouster and arrest, as authorities prepared to renew their efforts to detain him over his shor
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel helps former soldier leave Brazil over investigation into alleged war crimes in Gaza
item.description: Israel has helped a former soldier leave Brazil after legal action was initiated against him by a group accusing Israelis of war crimes in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mednick And Wafaa Shurafa, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Austrian Chancellor Nehammer says he will resign after talks on forming a new government fail
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Austrian Chancellor Karl Nehammer said Saturday he will resign in the coming days after talks on forming a new government failed a second time.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Central American troops arrive in Haiti to reinforce mission to restore order
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — About 150 military police officers from Central America have arrived in Haiti to reinforce the embattled government's fight against violent gangs that have upended daily life for millions in the Caribbean country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Royal family pays tribute to Prince William, Harry's nanny who lost stepson in New Orleans attack
item.description: Prince William shared his condolences to the family of his former nanny after her stepson was killed in the New Year’s terror attack in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: Bipartisan lawmakers renew push for legislation supporting Georgia against Russian interference
item.description: Republican and Democratic lawmakers are renewing efforts to pass legislation supporting Georgia, touting it as one of the best and first examples of bipartisan unity of the new Congress. Reps. Joe Wilson (R-S.C.) and Steve Cohen (D-Tenn.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy says Trump could be ‘decisive’ in bringing an end to the war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he thinks President-elect Donald Trump could be "decisive" in ending the nearly three-years-long war and dealing with Russian President Vladimir Putin's demands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 30 people killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza
item.description: At least 30 people, including children, were killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes overnight and into Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea’s impeached president avoids arrest attempt after hourslong standoff
item.description: Impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol avoided an arrest attempt Friday after an hourslong standoff between anti-corruption investigators and presidential security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes across Gaza kill at least 50, including several kids
item.description: Israeli airstrikes killed at least 50 people across central and southern Gaza, including an attack on a sprawling tent camp that Israel has repeatedly bombed despite designating it a humanitarian safe zone.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope calls for commitment to protect life as he doubles down on abortion in New Year's Day message
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis ushered in the New Year with a renewed appeal for the faithful to reject abortion, calling for a “firm commitment” to protect and respect life from conception to natural death. Francis, 88, celebrated a New Year’s Day Mass in St.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel kills Hamas commander who led heinous Oct. 7 attack on Kibbutz Nir Oz killed in drone attack: IDF
item.description: The Israel Defense Force announced that they had successfully killed one of the terrorist leaders who was responsible for the Oct. 7 attack on Israel in a targeted attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Migrants accused of killing Mexican border agent after he asked for their IDs: officials
item.description: Three suspected migrants were arrested Monday for allegedly killing a Mexican immigration agent who had asked to see their identification documents, Mexican officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 66 people die after a truck plunges into a river in southern Ethiopia
item.description: At least 66 people were killed in southern Ethiopia after an overcrowded truck that was contracted by wedding guests plunged into a river.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US levels sanctions on entities in Iran, Russia over attempted election interference
item.description: The Biden administration announced new sanctions against Russia and Iran on Tuesday in response to efforts by both countries to influence the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Palestinian official predicts Trump will 'destroy' Iran, leading to breakdown of remaining Hamas cells: report
item.description: Palestinian official Mohammad Hamdan said he believes President-elect Donald Trump will "destroy Iran" and effectively decimate Hamas' grasp on the region.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arrest warrant issued for impeached South Korean president as political crisis deepens
item.description: A South Korean court approved arrest warrants on Tuesday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol and search his office and residence amid a criminal investigation into his martial law decree.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine: How the war shifted in 2024
item.description: A third year of war in Ukraine drags on as the new year and the conflict's February anniversary approach. The war in 2024 saw major shifts on the battlefield and in Western politics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
